description:
They did not help me feel ready for a job and their other assistance felt like they wasted my time. I regret signing up with them despite having paid nothing to do so.

Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc is hidden gem with great course to expand skills to develop towards a workplace.

I believe being patience with each team member is very important as they all instruments as team leading, directing, guiding each client to serve each one as best as they can with the tools they are given to provide each client.

I will keep expanding my skills with this company as I am very happy with what I have learned so far; I have moments with an consultant I choose to be direct and firm to connect with the team to receive what I am seeking to achieve the career goals.

I wish Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc will receive renewed positive feedback in the future because I see the good, the potential, And I see area of improvement in this service for  who all seek this program to build the skills toward resilience for change for the business, the employees, and every client because each person is working hard towards their goals to achieve in success in many ways.

God bless.

I can't express enough gratitude for the incredible support I received from Performance Plus Rehabilitation Care Inc. (PPRC). They not only played a pivotal role in helping me to retain employment, but also went above and beyond by offering unwavering support, personalized skill training and constant encouragement. Their commitment to empowering individuals with disabilities extends beyond job placement - they have helped me to learn how to advocate for my rights including the right for reasonable accommodation so that I can sustainably participate in the workforce. Being able to participate in the workforce will directly lead to an increase in security and stability for my young family. They also taught me how to navigate the professional landscape with optimism, focus and confidence. I'm immensely thankful for their dedicated team and the positive impact they make. I would highly recommend this organization for their transformative approach, authenticity and genuine care!

It took some time to get my first appointment, but it was totally worth it. They helped me revamp my cv, create a solid cover letter, and find/apply for jobs. They helped me get a good job in the end. It wasn't instant, it depends what field your interested in. I got several different case workers over my time there and some were better than others, but most were very good. Don't expect to get a job on the first day and it can take several months to get the right job for you. That's just the nature of the job market. As I am sure you noticed, it is very hard to find good work these days. It can takes months before organizations even reply to you with an interview invite, and many organizations won't reply at all. You just have to be persistent. That's the reality of the modern job market. I stayed with them after I got a job with their help and eventually they connected me with a job in my field of interest.

Written Dec 2023

In Feb/April 2023 I came to realize that I was on the autism spectrum. And in May I began working with PPRC. What followed was a mix of incompetence, indifference and utter lack of empathy that has left me worse off than when I began.

Some notable issues include:
- An excessively long process to get to Job Development. Which after researching other providers, I have learned is atypical.
- Resumes that were incorrectly formatted or had spelling errors.
- No support from my job developer. Either in the form of leads or follow-ups
- Emails that were not replied to. Vital questions that were never answered.

Those are just the top-line examples I have many others. What made the experience so awful, was that I assumed PPRC and its management cared about helping disabled people find sustainable work. In all my time dealing with them I felt like a bother, an annoyance and that they did not care, whatsoever, about helping me or my situation.

I have no doubt, whatsoever that had I either worked with a different provider or on my own I would not be in the current situation I find myself in.

Avoid PPRC and find another provider. They are out there.

name: Canadian Hearing Services - Toronto and York Region
address: 271 Spadina Rd Toronto, ON, M5R 2V3 (400km) Clipboard
description:
I had a good experience here. I went in for a free hearing test during a promotion years ago.

My hearing tested “above average,” which surprised me because oftentimes my comprehension is so far off and/or delayed when people talk to me. When I mentioned confusion due to noticeable impairment, the staff member conducting my hearing test suggested the issue could be more about how I process sound than how I hear it. I didn’t understand what she meant at the time, but the comment stuck with me over the years.

I have since realized that I experience sensory input (e.g. auditory processing), emotions (e.g. emotional processing), and cognitive (e.g. attention and memory) differences as a neurodivergent (ND) individual. Looking back now, I really appreciate how insightful her comment was.

It was impossible to know the Labour Disruption which would affect our programs.  On May 3, 2025, over 130 participants registered the workshop, "Hearing Loss and Mental Health".  The guest speaker from your organization did not show up without any notice or apology.  Moreover, the unprofessional matters had happened again - two arranged hearing screening clinic days on May 8 and 9 were cancelled without any notice.  I MUST contacted all registered participants individually.  Unbelievable!!! I truly respect the right of the labour disruption.  However, your organization mentioned at the website officially, "We will continue to provide other services on a priority basis.  As always, the interest of our clients and staff are at the heart of all decisions that impact the operation of our organization."  Is it a big joke???

I recently was assessed at CHS referred by a friend. I was astonished at how organized and professional all the staff were. From the admin at the reception offering me a tissue when she noticed my nose running before I did to the overwhelmingly detailed and comprehensive audiology exam with Maria (who was also supervising a university student) the team was polite, empathetic and welcoming. The facility I went to on Spadina was very pleasant and comfortable. As a non-profit there was absolutely no sales emphasis - in fact I was told that aids were not advisable at this time. As an experienced health care worker I have no reservations recommending CHS.

UPDATE:  CHS reached out to me after seeing my review to understand what happened and I am happy with the conversations that occured and the resolution.  I have changed the stars to reflect their response and appreciate that they reached out.

Summary of Original Post: I signed my child up for the Basic Vocab for Youth Course and it was ok but class never ran the full time and there was an excessive amounts of breaks. I then thought give it one more chance with the 101 course and ended up dropping it after the first class as the teacher's set up made it hard to see her and the workbook required a DVD player to be able to do the exercises.  I did not receive acknowledgement from the office when we withdrew from the course and was not impressed with the lack of communication.

Years ago this was a great place to learn ASL. Offered summer immersion courses, had amazing teachers (all were Deaf) and in-person classes. Now only on-line.    Reached out several times to request return to in-person learning since I struggle to learn in a virtual environment. Been going to Bob Rumball for years due to this PLUS I get the impression that the Deaf community is not impressed with CHS. They really dropped the ball.

I am appalled and deeply disgusted by the woeful mismanagement at Community Living Cambridge. It is an establishment that claims to provide support for individuals with developmental disabilities, but in reality, it serves as a breeding ground for discrimination, neglect, and an absolute lack of empathy. The management's incompetence and indifference towards the needs and safety of the residents is nothing short of abhorrent.

First and foremost, it is abundantly clear that the management lacks the fundamental tools, resources, and skills necessary to handle individuals with special needs. They seem utterly oblivious to the fact that each resident requires tailored care and attention. Instead, they adopt a one-size-fits-all approach, conveniently brushing off the unique challenges and requirements faced by those under their supposed care.

The level of discrimination that runs rampant within Community Living Cambridge is utterly disgraceful. Rather than fostering an inclusive environment that nurtures growth and independence, the management fosters an atmosphere of exclusion and marginalization. Residents are subjected to favoritism, unequal treatment, and their voices are repeatedly silenced. It is deeply disturbing to witness a so-called support center propagate such discriminatory practices, effectively stripping individuals of their dignity and self-worth.

Even more troubling is the management's alarming indifference towards the abuse suffered by the vulnerable individuals entrusted to their care. Reports of neglect, mistreatment, and exploitation have fallen on deaf ears, as the management shamefully turns a blind eye to the pleas for help. It is unfathomable to comprehend how an organization that supposedly exists to support and protect these individuals can so callously disregard their cries for assistance.

Community Living Cambridge's management showcases a complete lack of compassion, empathy, and understanding for the people they are meant to serve. They are quick to accept their paychecks but fail to fulfill their responsibilities in a manner that promotes the well-being of the residents. It is an insult to the noble cause of caring for individuals with developmental disabilities.

In conclusion, the management at Community Living Cambridge is an abysmal failure. Their inability to provide proper care, their discriminatory practices, and their nonchalant approach towards abuse are nothing short of despicable. It is a place that should be avoided at all costs, as it clearly demonstrates a complete disregard for the welfare and rights of those with special needs. The residents deserve better than this wretched excuse for management.

description:
Catering Review:
It has taken me 2 months to decide to write this review, I had been reluctant as I had eaten at
The Lunchbox Café often. The food and service were always excellent, and I got to know Cheryl on a personal level.

I first reached out to Cheryl in Jan.2025, to secure her services for our event. We discussed date, location, delivery and the types and quantity of platters that we were considering based on our estimated number of attendees for our mother’s 90th Open House, July 19th.

After talking with my brother who has worked in the restaurant business his entire career, we placed our order, confident that we would get the same quality and service that I had experienced at the Café.

Over the next few months, we communicated back and forth sorting out the details of the order.
The order consisted of 4 lg. assorted sandwich trays, 20 wraps, 4 lg. veggie trays and 4 lg. cheese & crackers tray. I was told the sandwiches would assorted cut in quarters and the wraps would be cut in halves mixed in with the sandwiches for a nice presentation.

We were extremely disappointed with the order! Some of the trays were obviously damaged during delivery, squished messy sandwiches, the sandwich trays were NOT assorted as promised, and the wraps were on a separate tray, not mixed in the sandwiches! The sandwiches and wraps were slimy and falling apart, obviously not prepared on the day of the event.  The veggies were not fresh and crackers on the cheese trays had already begun to soften. We had to find new trays, luckily there were some at the facility and do our best to create our own assortments and reform the sandwiches and wraps (Cheryl provided us with gloves to do this! on her delivery) obviously knowing they were not going to be presentable for our guests.

I followed up in writing to Cheryl, to let her know how disappointed we were. This was her response,
“Yes, sandwiches were made day before-we are a weekday program so no staff on weekends -just me. Given sandwiches were cut in quarters – should have used more trays because they just don’t stay together when cut in quarters. Veggies were purchased on the Thursday, crackers were put on cheese trays the morning of. Thanks for the feedback”

We feel we should have been made aware of all these factors in advance, to make an informed choice of caterers. We were embarrassed and any leftovers had to be disposed of because they were not fresh enough to keep.

Schouten Family.

WOW Cheryl...your Christmas fruit cake, like no other!! So moist and full of fruit.

Unfortunately CLHN is the only day "program" in Milton, so clients have no other choices.  The Clients are bored most of the day. Unless they leave the premises for an outing that costs extra. They often sit and stare into space. Families have spoken out and asked for better services but it has fallen on deaf ears. My daughter has developed anxiety from having no direction or interaction for most of the day.  Thank goodness for her friends there and for the Purple Stage door and Yoga.  The Majority of families have no choice but to send their loved ones and are afraid to speak out.  It's a disgrace. There are some lovely workers, it's just a shame they are stuck in a place that is so lacking for quality.

The new location was great (at least there were windows) but short lived - they are closing for summer.  New activities in the community started but that also is closing.

While clients are in-house (which is the better part of their day - everyday) they are not engaged.  They are left to their own accord to entertain themselves.  Often they are starring  off into space or twirling around the room in boredom.  The days are long and they are ready to leave before the time is up.  They wait by the window for their ride.

There’s no reason it couldn’t be made into a positive healthy environment that they look forward to going to, where they could thrive - a place they wouldn’t want to leave.

Why is that too much to ask ?
Is it because clients don’t have other options for Milton?
No effort is required?

What is advertised and the reality are quite different.  The photos look great online.  I assume they were moments in a larger scope of time. Perhaps that’s where all the effort goes to - marketing.

The in-house engagement between paid programming (which is above and beyond CLNH fees)   is concerning.  It’s non-existent completely.

If you can afford CLNH daily fee PLUS fees for every single program offered through the day and send in your own entertainment for your loved ones for between the paid programming - it could be doable but you are basically paying full day fee for your loved one to just sit in a room.

They can’t even participate in events that are about them (ie. Flag raising at Town Hall). What a great opportunity for inclusiveness and celebration.

Who is all the advertising for???
The business or the people ????

Hot dogs and Kraft dinner today for the $5 per person lunch they charge.  Disgusting.  It's so unfortunate families don't have another option for their loved ones.

name: Youth Employment Services YES
address: 1610 Bloor St W Toronto, ON, M6P 1A7 (25km) Clipboard
description:
I went to this office for information on Better Jobs Ontario. The orientation went smooth enough. However, speaking to Zarah was a very exhausting experience. She continued stalling and giving conflicting information on whether or not the ministry would pay for my courses. I had to end the call before I lost it.

The next day I went to complain to the manager Diane/Diana & she stated she wouldn`t talk to or take action against Zarah since she was 'doing her job'. I don`t know about you but I heard of people who were just 'doing their job'.

THIS PLACE IS A WASTE. AVOID THIS OFFICE.

***UPDATE 26 FEBRUARY 2026***

I was told by Florencia Boronat that I`d be contacted by the Vice President on the week of 26 Feb. I haven`t heard anything from them all week until I had to email them. I feel that since I had to remind them of a situation they take 'seriously', they said the VP would contact me later that day. I figured they put a 'rush' on trying to get this situation dealt with.

Later the VP (Carrie-Anne Goodfellow) did call me as promised. I was (as expected) given the brush off by her. She said (I`m paraphrasing) 'If you didn`t like our service, please go to another office'. There was no real remedy to resolve the issue at hand which was difficult representatives, conflicting information & managers who refuse to take action. Every time I tried to air my grievances, Carrie-Ann kept trying to interrupt or talk over me. This is behavior unbecoming of a Vice President of any business or charity. When someone needs to vent or air their griefs, the best thing to do is listen more than you talk. I don`t thing Carrie-Ann got that memo. She even went on to say 'We are under funded'. This has nothing to do with the issue at hand. If anything, this is a weak excuse to try to dismiss the situation. She even tried to get off the call while I was complaining. I hope Carrie-Ann`s boss reads this review.

The only good thing (if any) about this is that I complained about 3 other offices and this is the ONLY one that had the professional courtesy to contact me. Still, they did the bare minimum & it`s going to hurt business wise in the long run.

THIS OFFICE IS ONE SAD JOKE.

Awful experience as a job seeker here. I don't know how this place remains in operation. They have to find a way to justify government funding, when in reality they are of little use. I never received proper info on 'start-up cost' assistance which used to be handled by OW, despite repeated requests. This is a reflection of widespread dysfunction across Toronto social services. My worker, Desmond, repeatedly delayed answering emails, (once as long as three weeks!) and always had an excuse. Lack of follow-through for job interviews and no response from management when I e-mailed a complaint. Leaving them was a nightmare too: I had to get an OW supervisor to close my account with them! Stay far away from this place, unless you want to waste your time and be continually frustrated!

with the way phone calls are handled at your office. Unfortunately, the receptionist often does not answer phone calls at all, and when we leave voice messages, no one ever calls back.

name: META Vocational Services Inc
address: 15 Thickson Rd N, Unit 6 Whitby, ON, L1N 8W7 (9km) Clipboard
description:
I have brought a few individuals to META over the last few months. I don’t generally leave Google reviews, but the team at META has been so friendly, non-judgemental, and professional. We have had the pleasure of working with Michelle and she has went above and beyond! I really can’t say enough. Kind, knowledgeable, resourceful, patient, creative…I could go on. What I’ve noticed about the individuals, as the time goes by, is that they have been able to be fully themselves and they feel heard. On a personal note, as a person who is results driven, it has been such a Blessing to be able to see the amount of work that has been accomplished in such a short period of time.

Emily Smith is an Employment Counsellor at Meta’s Whitby location.  I have been using their services for many years and have worked with numerous counselors. All were good-all were very professional, but I would have to say Ms. Smith is the best.  She is very patient. When you schedule an appointment, she gives you enough time to express your concerns, and is very quick in finding a solution. Also, you immediately know how knowledgeable she is regarding the quickest path to finding a job.  For example, the process of constructing a revised resume and cover letter. After just one appointment, she was able to assist in improving both . She knew the best template to use and made them a lot more concise.  I consider Ms. Smith to be a great communicator and a great motivational speaker. There were times when I felt pessimistic in the pursuit of finding a better higher paying job, but her encouragement really helped. And on May 24, 2020, I was able to get a higher paying job. My previous job was only minimum wage, but now my current job pays over 20 an hour.

Thank You Emily

META Services has been an exceptional and incredibly helpful resource for me. Firstly, they did a phenomenal job in putting together and updating my resume. They did an incredible job with this. Also, my last contract position I found through their help and assistance and am very greatful for that. I appreciate all they have done for me and all the assistance they have provided. Emily has been extremely helpful and a pleasure to work with. Thank you so much for all your assistance!!  Bruce Patterson.

Hi Leanne, I am so grateful for you to help me find a job and for all your support you provided me.  You’ve worked really hard and I am very excited to start work.

META is a place to go if you want to increase your chance of finding a job. The staff is amazing and always go above and beyond to help you. Sherry has been helping me, my resume looks much better and I have picked up very useful tips for giving interview. I highly recommend META!

description:
I had the pleasure of learning about Community Living Ajax-Pickering and Whitby, and I just want to take a moment to share how truly impressed I am by the incredible work they do. 💙

This organization is all about creating an inclusive and supportive community for individuals with developmental disabilities, and you can really feel the passion they put into everything. From supportive services to creating meaningful opportunities for people to get involved, they go above and beyond to ensure everyone feels valued and empowered.

What really stood out to me was the genuine care and dedication of the staff. They’re not just providing services—they’re making a real difference in people’s lives, helping them build independence and become an active part of our community.

If you’re looking for a way to give back or get involved with an organization that truly cares, Community Living Ajax-Pickering and Whitby is a local gem. Whether through volunteering, supporting events, or simply spreading awareness, this is an organization worth supporting. 🌟

Thank you to everyone at Community Living for your continued dedication to making our community a better, more inclusive place! 🙏

name: Second Chance Employment Counselling
address: 435 Stone Rd W, Suite 213 Guelph, ON, N1G 2X6 (18km) Clipboard
description:
Had an appointment with Madison. Very engaging and friendly and genuinely seemed like someone who likes helping people. My situation wasn't for the basic job searching, I already have 2 decades of that experience in Guelph already, but something more specific. Hopefully the next step will go well so I can re-train for a career.

Our child/ youth really needed a second chance at life. They came in to this place multiple times for help, they were fully engaged.

However, Second Chance was non responsive to our child/ youth the whole time. They eventually shut down our child saying “you have autism so you’re disqualified,” from one of their main programs. This had a devastating impact on our child/youths life.

When I reported this to their manager, did they care? Not at all. In fact, they used their descent communication skills to speak diplomatically to “smooth it all over.” So, there was no acknowledgment, no apology, and certainly no improvement being made with this company.

As a tax paying social service professional in this community, I found the conduct of this company (Norfolk location) disappointing and concerning.

I recieved excellent assistance from Gabriel today!
He was very kind to everyone he interacted with and seemed glad to help by answering the questions I had about updating my resume for the position I'm applying for.

Gisella Huggins has been an absolute God sent for me at this time in my life.  Her beautiful presence and resourcefulness and understanding has been an incredible blessing.  She goes above and beyond to help guide me in this unchartered territory to help me create the new life I imagine with grace and ease.  I am infinitely grateful for her as i figure out my new career journey.  I feel so blessed to have her guidance and support. Thank you Gisella for your beautiful light and heart presence!

I have awesome experience with Second Chance, a legit job counselling organization. Sarah and Osman connected me to my first employer in Canada, and for that I'm always grateful. Up to date, I'm still working closely with the team to ensure my job goals are attained. Long live Second Chance.

description:
Thank you to our coordinator Julie B for being compassionate, understanding of our families needs for needed respite care to support our toddler as well. Julie was patient and did the application process while explaining each part of the application.

We’ve benefitted from the SSAH funding for respite care which has reduced our stress and helped us do more for our daughter.

Thank you and we truly appreciate the understanding of our needs.

Julie also responds to emails easily and communicates with our KidsAbility social worker which is truly amazing.

We appreciate the referral from our social worker at KidsAbility.

Thank you

A psychiatrist there wrote a mean incriminating report against our vulnerable son. Then their overly ridged document control officer wouldn’t let anyone see the report but our child/ youth. This (CMHA) caused our child their first major mental health break down.

It was a long 2 year wait for eating disorder help for our child, which eventually panned out to nothing. The counsellor our child was assigned in the mean time was manipulative, unprofessional and basically had qualifications at the level of an intake worker.

Did they care? Not at all. In fact (youth hub aside), Guelph CMHA (counsellors and especially their child/youth manager) repeatedly acted very complacent, disengaged and non caring of the vulnerable youth needing help in our Guelph community.

As a tax paying and social service professional in the Guelph community, I found the overall conduct of CMHA Guelph Wellington, especially for our struggling youth, fairly disappointing and concerning.

So I waited the time for a call which was about 1-2 years, nothing. So I called to see what was going on, thinking it was extended, &  was told that there is in fact NO waitlisting and they don't do waitlisting! So I never got any care or attention other than a Lorazepam from the hospital. Thanks CMHA!

I am a patient of Dr. Kasbia and I find him to be very easy to work on my mental health issues, he listens and helps you find the right way through my complex problems. He has helped me to find healthy ways to help my brain find a good way to operate. I have also participated in many learning groups  and in those groups I have been taught ways to train my brain to respond  to anxiety and disassociation issues. Some that I participated in are ERG which is emotional regulation group and DBT  and I have learned so much.
I am very grateful for this health care service.

He explained my next step would be to come in and register. I went into the office today to complete my registration. I met with Kenton and cannot say enough about what a positive experience it was. It is evident this is a organization who genuinely cares about their applicants and will assist in anyway to ensure they are successful in their future career endeavours.

This has to be one of the friendliest businesses  to visit in Lindsay, if you are looking for employment. I had the pleasure of working with Karen S. At our first meeting I gave her my old resume and she tweaked it and sent it to an employer I was interested in working for. I got a call and interview and a new job within a couple of weeks. The programs are set up to lift you up, get you ready and seeing that you have what you need to be successful.

I couldn’t say enough positive things about this business in our community. Take a look and take advantage of what’s offered. Nothing better then to be set up to succeed.
A.C.

They didn't understand the eligibility requirements themselves and months were wasted with my ultimately still not receiving logical explanations for anything. If this agency is responsible for helping locals get back into the employment stream then I can't imagine what those statistics look like.

description:

Dealing with ServiceOntario is rarely fun, but the Lindsay location on Kent Street West does a decent job of making it as painless as possible for a government office. It’s centrally located and handles everything from health cards (OHIP) to driver's licenses and vehicle permits.
The Good: The main thing this location has going for it is accessibility and location. It's fully wheelchair accessible, has a lower-level counter, and offers free parking around the building, which is huge in downtown Lindsay. The staff, for the most part, are professional and knowledgeable, and they offer a full range of essential services for the Kawartha Lakes area. They are often quick to process routine transactions if you have all your documents ready.
The Not-So-Good: Like any ServiceOntario, wait times can be a real headache, especially if you try to walk in during a busy lunch hour or on a Monday morning. The information warns that walk-ins are limited due to high demand, so you’ll definitely want to check the website or call ahead to see if you can do your transaction online first. Also, sometimes different staff members seem to have slightly inconsistent requirements for documentation, which is frustrating if you have to make a second trip.

Finally, I can write a purely positive post for this town today. Trust me, I don't like having to write reviews outing places and their rule breaking. However there is no need for that thinking here, as this place has gone more than above the "call" for what is going on with all my cases. Personally, most times these CMHA buildings (at least around the GTA) have an overload of clients and thus only do the bare minimum. Not this sector of Mental Health, they remind us why mental health is important, even to the common individual.

Where do I begin complimenting this specific building and its workers? The receptionists are well spoken and know how to handle many different kinds of individuals who do come in, varying from minimal to severe cases. They are efficient in doing their job at the front desk. As for the crisis worker section, the two workers I had seen has helped on separate occasions where I needed to sort out thoughts as I do have high-functioning Asperger's Syndrome and the way these neurons fire rapidly, need to speak out my overwhelmed mind so I can process on my own what is the most viable option to proceed, especially everything that's going on in the courts and my personal life at the moment. On top of this, their specialized workers are phenomenal! I personally have already learned everything this organization has sanctioned over all of Ontario, so I tend to grind against these specialists as I am on par with their intellectual studies as I myself am a "knowledge sponge". Not this one worker, she was able to break through and gained my trust as I do not give that away care free. On top of this, even when there was one occasion where there was conflict between me and a worker due to opposing views, the both of us were able to come to terms and to keep our philosophical beliefs from hindering me the help from this worker.

This place should get much more recognition, and personally think that this specific branch of CMHA should get a budget increase to ensure that people have a reliable place to go to, rather you be a common individual going through a hard time or a person diagnosed with mental impairments. God bless this organization and I hope that you never falter from your ethics. Your organization brought hope and help to a person who thought his life was over due to the excessive abuse he's gone through.

10/5 stars for this place, if it was feasible!

But without notice my worker had been switched. After waiting 4 business day after leaving multiple messages again for them. I had to go to the office in person. Having physical limitations and mobility issues. As well as having surgery 3 weeks ago. This was extremely difficult. By far the worst office in the city!

As an ODSP recipient, I often feel overlooked and unheard. I struggle to get timely responses from my caseworker through the MyBenefits portal, where my inquiries often go unanswered. After waiting several days, I have no choice but to call, only to encounter further barriers.

When I call the main ODSP line, I am sometimes given the option to leave a voicemail, but the mailbox isn’t even set up. This forces me to call back and wait on hold for up to 45 minutes, only to be met with a staff member who is dismissive, speaks over me, and only takes a message for my caseworker. I am then told I will receive a call back within a day, but in reality, the wait is often much longer.

This process becomes an exhausting cycle. Despite long delays, I am repeatedly denied access to a backup caseworker. This is especially frustrating because my caseworker's voicemail message specifically states that if they are unavailable, I should request assistance from a backup caseworker. Even when I have asked to speak with an office manager, I have not received any follow-up.

When I was first approved for ODSP, my caseworker would respond within hours and follow up the same day. However, after my caseworker changed, I have faced ongoing difficulties in communication and support.

Currently, I am experiencing significant delays with the processing of my Employee Start-Up Benefit. In the past, this benefit was processed within two days, yet my current request—submitted on February 21st, 2025—has now been pending for 18 days as of March 11th, 2025. I submitted all required documents and was informed that I was approved, with no additional paperwork needed. However, five days later, there has been no update, and I am struggling to get any answers from the MyBenefits platform, my caseworker, or anyone else.

Previously, I had to escalate my concerns to the Premier of Ontario and the Ministry of Children, Community, and Social Services just to receive a response. While I understand that ODSP caseworkers have a 24-hour call-back policy, waiting a week or more without any communication is unacceptable. If a caseworker is unavailable or on leave, a backup caseworker should step in to ensure that clients do not experience delays in accessing essential benefits.

People with disabilities deserve the same respect and courtesy that ODSP staff would expect in return. I urge ODSP leadership to re-train staff on the importance of timely communication, empathy, and accountability. We rely on these services for our well-being, and at the very least, we deserve the assurance that our concerns will be acknowledged and addressed in a professional and timely manner.

name: Michael Garron Hospital
address: 825 Coxwell Ave Toronto, ON, M4C 3E7 (9km) Clipboard
description:
Michael Garron Hospital is a well-regarded community teaching hospital in East Toronto, Ontario, that often gets positive recognition for its role in providing comprehensive, patient-centred care to a large and diverse local population.

People and organizations who speak about the hospital often highlight that:

It’s been serving the East Toronto community for nearly a century, caring for hundreds of thousands of residents with a full range of services — from emergency, maternal and newborn care to pediatric, surgery, mental health, and community programs.

The Stavro Emergency Department is busy and innovative, with features like an E-Booking portal for minor injuries and a child-friendly emergency zone that makes visits less stressful for families.

It’s recognized for high standards of care and safety, with Accreditation Canada awarding it exemplary standing, meaning it meets very high quality benchmarks.

The hospital is affiliated with the University of Toronto Faculty of Medicine and other institutions, which supports a teaching environment that keeps practices current and rooted in research.

The hospital has received community support and awards; for example, it’s been named one of the Greater Toronto’s Top Employers for multiple years — an indication of good workplace culture and how it supports staff and services.

Visitors and patients often share that MGH is compassionate and professional, especially in special areas like child care and maternity. While personal experiences vary just like at any major hospital, many families report that staff are caring and that children and newborns get thoughtful attention.

Overall, Michael Garron Hospital is seen as a trusted medical cornerstone for East Toronto — strong in clinical services, supportive of diverse patient needs, and continually evolving to meet community growth.

name: Ontario. Ministry of Children, Community and Social Services

description:
The worst Odsp office in Canada .  If you have any urgent questions or needs ,you won’t get a call back or answer if any kind for at least half a month if not a full month . Got transferred to this office in July, never had so frustrating a series of experiences before. Since then I still don't have a dedicated caseworker, have to juggle between 'backups', supervisors, the main workers, etc just to get anything done, and it's a fight just to get my income submissions approved, which I do like clockwork every month, but since nobody actually READS my stuff on Mybenefits, the system flags my file on hold every month due to 'not submitting' income information. Then, like clockwork, I end up playing phone tag for 45 minutes to an entire day just to get someone long enough to check my account for two minutes to unflag it. The government is supposed to help the disabled, not make the process even harder than when I was on Ontario Works...

Edit: I wanted to add that one worker, Usha, has been doing her best to juggle my needs in alongside her other clients even though I'm not her client or even backup client. The office is so severely understaffed that she has to take on more clients than usual officially, but also has to take on clients who aren't even hers and there isn't enough hours in the day to deal with the matters that need to be dealt with. I sympathize with her and the other workers despite the situation still being not completely resolved for myself. A lot of the issue is staffing related, but I'm grateful she's managed to pop in and help me when it feels like very few other workers there can spare the time.

I believe  this location  is over subscibed. Malvern  is a low income community and I know  for a fact,  it has  one of the highest rate of strokes  and other unique  medical  conditions in Scarborough. That means said the Malvern office needs more caseworkers to deal with the volume of work this office generates. I can only imagine how difficult it is for 50 case workers or even less to deal with the thousands of calls and follow-ups and as they're human sometimes their frustration shows . I think more funding is required at this location to increase Staffing and improve the phone system.  My advice go  in person or use a designate. Be patient.

name: Canadian Mental Health Association, Toronto Branch
address: Lawrence Square Mall, 700 Lawrence Ave W, Suite 480 Toronto, ON, M6A 3B4 (10km) Clipboard
description:
Dangerous Bell let’s talk messages

As a parent of a teenager, I’m deeply concerned about the increasing prevalence of negative mental health messaging around us. Schools already place immense pressure on teens, and they’re constantly exposed to violent video games, harsh music, and dark themes in media. What’s even more troubling are the frequent public ads stating alarming statistics, like “1 in 4 teens considers suicide.” These messages, while meant to raise awareness, can feel overwhelming and even counterproductive for young minds.

Recently, while watching basketball as a family to promote positivity and connection, we were interrupted by such an ad. It left us stunned and struggling to respond. Mental health campaigns must be handled with extreme caution, ensuring they uplift rather than unintentionally harm. I urge organizations to rethink their approach and prioritize hope, resilience, and solutions over fear.

Just amazing. The staff is so organized and professional. I love my first therapist I had there. She was attentive, caring, and so encouraging. She met my needs very well and I was able to be myself. My second therapist has also been amazing. I can tell she cares and she keeps telling me she wants to work with me constantly. The actual DBT program I am in is so inspiring and helpful. The groups are always laid out so well, the booklets are so detailed, and the lessons they give are always detailed and assistive. They respect individual impairments and are always respectful of your space and emotions. There is always drinks and sometimes food, as well as resources (markers, toys, things to play with), etc. Everything is comprehensive and it has been a joy to come here. I have never encounter a negative person, and when negative situations have come up, they have been supportive. Even the receptionist always says hi and ask how I am. They have a great job assistance program and it has helped me get my first job after getting on disability and valuable skills in that field. They help me sort my paperwork and remain productive. I love it here. Thank you so much for all the support from this place. I recommend through and through coming here for any of your needs. They offer even more programming than I mentioned and it is always loving here. Easily accessible and informative. I hope people have had a similar experience to me!

name: Wayne's Cup Café and Life Skills Centre
address: 9889 Markham Rd, Unit 4 Markham, ON, L6E 0B7 (16km) Clipboard
description:
Amazing little joint and a wonderful way to support the community ❤️. Brayden or Braden (sorry I didn’t catch the spelling) helped me out. I also met the owner /Wayne’s mum and she’s so kind!

Got myself a chai tea, cheese panini, tender chocolate chip cookie and a little goodie bag.

I recommend dropping by here to support this lovely initiative

Wayne's Cup is a small cafe with a very small seating area inside. There's a washroom for customers and staff only. In the back they have the kitchen, the life skills training area which is for students and staff only.

This cafe is a social enterprise that hires Autistic individuals and is also a life skills training centre. They are also a nonprofit.

All the baked goods and food are made in house. The coffee/tea, baked goods, and hot food are all decently priced, kind of cheaper than other cafes.

I ordered the Hot Vegetables Pizza Panini ($6.50). It came stuffed with cheese, peppers, onions and a few other vegetables within the panini. It was a tasty panini.

Would probably come back to try their baked goods and whatever Shelley's Hot Pot is. If they weren't so far away.

Wayne’s cup, which is a place I’ve never been to, but I’ve heard positive things about how they Helped adults with autism and their café food is pretty good But I’m glad they’re doing all the hard work to help these people in need. We need more organization, supporting people with autism.

Great coffee shop and bakery with wonderful service. Will definitely be back :)

The panini and cookie were amazing! Excellent place!

name: L'Arche Daybreak
address: 11339 Yonge St Richmond Hill, ON, L4S 1L1 (16km) Clipboard
description:
Ok, this review is going out for somebody who is a staff member from autism Ontario. Her name is Allie now I’m not naming the particular person because of the confidentiality reasons but this program which is located just a couple minutes away from where I live had actually adopted somebody who had autism and fetal alcohol syndrome which was the most respectful and most shocking thing I have never seen in my whole life also, this day program also takes good care of the adults with disabilities and they do a lot for the community which I am grateful for thankfully there’s somewhere at home that has this program. I would recommend anybody who has a person with a disability to check this program out support for people with disabilities does not end until you’re 21.

L’Arche Daybreak is more than a care community—it’s a place that brings deep meaning to the lives of everyone who visits their little farm in the heart of Richmond Hill. The community is warm, inclusive, and full of life. Be sure to stop by their art studio, where you’ll see incredible pieces created by core members, each telling a story of love and creativity. A visit to Daybreak will touch your heart in unexpected ways.

This place is amazing. It has a very nice chapel, a cottage look interior, and an very kind community. My school did a retreat here and it introduced/taught us many new things. Was very nice to spend a day here and to feel part of the community.

After reading the Road to Daybreak I set my sights on visiting. I felt compelled to visit because I wanted to devote myself to other people with disabilities.
I had a disability but it really didn’t matter to me.
I made the necessary arrangements and found myself in Toronto in January of all times to visit.
I spent a few days at the New House where Henri lived but he was away but I did spend time with Adam and all the other core members. One asked me if I was going to stay. I didn’t know. I wanted to but I had just suffered a major loss in my family and I felt the need to be with family.
I had planned to return but instead I worked with disabled people here in New Jersey.
I wasn’t looking for anything to do since I already graduated from college.  I Just felt like I had to visit and I wasn’t disappointed.

Lorrie

A well managed place for meetings and prayers. They’ve got a quiet pond.

name: Ontario. Ministry of Children, Community and Social Services
address: 340 Dufferin St Toronto, ON, M6K 1Z9 (16km) Clipboard
description:
Unfortunately, my experience has not been the best for the several times since they changed my caseworker. I rarely need them, but when I do need them, they never answer the phone, and they don't give any call back for voice messages. They never answer the phone; music plays for over 20 minutes no matter the time you call, and they pass you to another extension to leave a message, which they never answer. They should change the message to "Let us hold you forever." It doesn't matter if you need to talk to someone; they always say the person is not available this week, but I've been calling for more than 2 weeks, and the person is still not available. I asked when the caseworker comes, and they replied, "I don't know," and if I forward to a different caseworker, I have to leave another voice message, which is never replied to. And they advise that bad behavior is not allowed when they are the ones who have bad behavior.
"Remain on the line, and your call will be answered in sequence," and they recognize the number, and they hang up the call.

Hello,

I would like to share an important concern regarding the reception services.

I have observed that some front desk staff members do not always treat individuals with disabilities with the patience, understanding, and respect they deserve. At times, their attitude may seem dismissive or unwilling to assist, which can make people feel uncomfortable or unwelcome.

As the reception desk is the first point of contact, it is very important that everyone — especially individuals with disabilities — is treated with kindness, empathy, and professionalism.

I kindly request that this matter be reviewed and that appropriate awareness or sensitivity training be provided to ensure a more inclusive, respectful, and supportive environment for everyone.

You call on the phone, but no one answers. You text ‘my benefit,’ but still no one replies. I don’t understand.”

This office has been the bane of my existence for years. The staff treat everyone as if they are stupid, which is discriminatory at best. They never answer the phone, they take a minimum of 10 days to respond to any online communications, and they respond by saying whatever question/issue you have is not their problem and to deal with it yourself. Dealing with this office is like pulling teeth. Unprofessional, unkind, disrespectful, and ableist staff all around. Advocating for yourself is difficult enough without people treating you like you're stupid just because you're disabled. The most frustrating part of any day is if I have to deal with this office in any capacity.

I have been on ODSP for almost ten years and have dealt with many offices throughout Ontario.

This location is THE WORST location I have interacted with due to the level of unprofessionalism, lack of communication and lack of empathy from staff members.

Yes, there are a handful of workers who are respectful, have integrity and take their job seriously (thank you!). Unfortunately the good ones are outnumbered and overshadowed by those who couldn’t care less about their performance and recipients lives.

It’s ironic, the same people those “workers“ don’t want to assist, is the one and only reason their employment exists!

Maybe I’m wrong but it seems the math is: mess with your mental health = job security.🤷‍♀️

ODSP only approves people with mental health disorders and not people with actually disabilities or injuries that will keep them out of work for over a year. I myself had Dr Eardly who is the head doctor of the Toronto Marlies Hockey team write up detailed letter to ODSP stating my Injuries will prevent me from working for a minimum of 1 year. I am a brick and stone mason, which is one of the most labor intensive jobs in the world and ODSP thought I could still work with  a torn meniscus,  frozen shoulder and a hernia.

Now, because I know people that have fraudulent doctors that have writen up lies that can not be empirically proven get approved for ODSP, I was told by an acquaintance that she could get me in contact with someone that will fill out all my forms for me to make sure I get approved.

I declined because fraud is fraud and my good conscious would not allow me to do such a thing beacuse God see's everything.

I get it there there is a budget and you can not approve everyone that applies. But when you know there is an abundance of fraudulent applications getting approved and then there are people that legitimately need it, get declined its just another sign that people in charge of ODSP do not care about clear, concise and accurate information. Public servants of today do nothing but help fraudulent activity flourish while turning their backs on the ones that actually need it.

God bless and thank you for taking the time to read my review.

Excellent top notch, great work sessions, great customer service.

If you're looking for employment opportunities and you're not getting any interviews/or jobs then I would highly recommend setting up and appointment with one of Woodgreen's highly skilled Employment Consultants to take a second look at your Resume and polish it off so you can get the job that you want!

name: YWCA Hamilton
address: 75 MacNab St S Hamilton, ON, L8P 3C1 Clipboard
description:
The YWCA is a wonderful place that helps women. But I do think they need to re train their male security guards when it comes to boundaries with women and personal space. It is not appropriate for male guards to hover inches behind vulnerable women without their knowledge or consent. It can be extremely triggering for somone who has been abused. Personal space matters. Respect for boundaries matters.

I especially love this place because it is dedicated to serving women and the elderly, great! I fell in love with the RANA coaching staff since last Friday, and there is an hour-long free course this Friday at 12:00～1:00time

The supervised access center is very disorganized and is very hard to get into contact with. I believe that Natalie is in charge of the supervised access center and she never answers her phone. There has been numerous times that I have had to have the YCWA receptionist send emails to her just to be called back by a “No Caller ID” that I mistaken for spam. I am in court to get visitation with my child and the judge granted me approval to get supervised access, which is supposed to upgrade to unsupervised access after a certain number of visits. It’s been 6+ months and I haven’t been able to get 1 visit done because I cannot reach her. They haven’t even completed my ex wife’s registration yet and I’ve already payed for both of our registration fees! Ive spent over $13,000 dollars in court/lawyer fees because we keep having to make new settlement conference dates because I am not meeting my visitation requirements due to their lack of care.

A few years ago I started going there, and was the best experience ever. Equipment is ok but the workers that were there were the top notch. I went there not only to lose weight but it actually cleared my mental mind. It was an oasis for me. I loved every minute of it. Any of the staff were there to help you at all times. Some of the staff were knowledgeable in mental illness. They were trained well because the ones I spoke with actually helped me. It absolutely sucks that this place is not a safe place I  go to anymore. They closed  . Most of all it kept my mind clear, better than any medication I've been on in my 40 something years. I am so upset and devastated that this place is not open for us anymore. My life is destroyed again but if you guys open up again that would be the best thing to ever happen to me and I'm sure so many other woman an hour community.

Floor staff are great, in gym and pool, good people.

Policies and service from "the office" leave much to be desired. Also they seem to clean house on support staff for the shelter every other year, and im not confident in their priorities regarding the use of funding.

name: VPI Employment Services
address: 1051 Upper James St, Ste 102 Hamilton, ON, L9C 3A6 (4km) Clipboard
description:
Life changed for me recently in a big way and VPI employment services has been a huge relief to me. My counselor Fathima has been working closely with me to help me develop my skills, get my confidence back and to help me be job ready. This past week both Fathima and Mishal have been running a workshop called Power of Possible for clients like myself and it has been extremely helpful! Just some of the things I've been learning is how to identify my skills, being prepared for tough interview questions, strategies on managing money and how to be assertive. VPI offers a lot of tools and resources to help build your confidence and get you ready to be apart of Canada's work force. Whether if you're new to Canada or just have been out of the job market for a while, they can offer you something to help you get on your career path. It's also great to know that once you get a job they still work with you to make sure you have the tools to be successful. I wasn't aware of such places like VPI prior to my life changes. I wish I had or I could of made some changes sooner! I'm excited to see what my future has in store thanks to them!

I want to express my deepest gratitude to Abby, who accompanied me for several months during my job search. She guided me with patience and dedication on how to improve my resume and all the details needed to secure a position.
I also want to sincerely thank Mishal, who with an excellent attitude supported me in obtaining a voucher to buy the clothing and essentials I needed to start my new job.
Thank you to VPI Hamilton, because right now many of us truly need support to begin working. The help you provide to purchase essentials, clothing, shoes, boots, etc, is invaluable.
A thousand thanks, and may you continue with this excellent work that so many people need.

I am very thankful to VPI. They support me well.

Specifically i would like to say thanks n pay salute to my Counselor Fathima Sincy, she is very humble,  supportive n kind hearted lady. She listens me with great patience n give the best solutions.
May Almighty God bless her n her family too Amen🙏.

This review is based on my cousin’s experience with VPI Employment Services, and it was terrible from start to finish. The receptionist was consistently rude and unprofessional, which made every visit stressful. There was no sense of welcome or willingness to help.

My cousin worked with Atta and his manager, and neither provided any real support. They didn’t follow up, didn’t offer useful guidance, and acted like my cousin was only there to help them meet their numbers. It genuinely felt like their priority was securing government funding, not helping people find jobs.

On top of that, once they open a file, they make it extremely difficult to close it or transfer to another employment centre. It traps people in a system that isn’t helping them at all.

Based on my cousin’s experience, I would strongly recommend avoiding this place and finding an employment service that actually cares about the people it’s supposed to support.

From day one, I was promised help, but after the initial conversation they stopped communicating and provided absolutely nothing. For three months, I received zero real support. I found a job entirely on my own, which makes their involvement pointless.

Only after I secured work without them did they suddenly start asking for my personal information, with no clear explanation. Total disorganization.

The entire experience felt chaotic, misleading, and incompetent. There was no follow-through, no accountability, and no respect for my time.

I asked them to close my file and clearly stated that they are not authorized to use or share my personal information, especially information connected to Work Anywhere. I will not deal with this company again.

I strongly discourage anyone from using this service.

Her patience, professionalism, encouragement, and genuine care made a significant difference in my experience. She took the time to understand my goals, provided personalized guidance, and remained supportive and responsive throughout the entire process. Her commitment truly went above and beyond.

Through Agilec’s guidance—and Shweta’s unwavering support—I gained not only practical tools but also renewed confidence, clarity, and motivation in pursuing meaningful employment. Their encouragement strengthened my belief in my abilities and helped me move forward with purpose.

I highly recommend Agilec to anyone seeking employment support, career development, or job-readiness training. They are doing remarkable work by empowering individuals to rebuild confidence and successfully transition into new opportunities.

Shweta has been an absolute best and very supportive throughout the entire process. She has helped me with interview preparation, documentation and logistics, and is always responsive and quick. I really appreciate the support in helping me find secure and meaningful employment, thank you Shweta!

description:
I had the occasion to come to this address to attend a meeting. It houses two different organisations that provide support for individuals with disabilities that limit their mobility.

I was immediately struck by the building's marvellous brickwork and it's long and low structure. I thought at first it was a heritage building from Kingston's industrial past. However, the meeting's host explained that, yes, the building had an industrial background but it was a relatively recent construction. Furthermore, the building's interior had recently been completely gutted in order to house the numerous offices and meeting rooms required by its new inhabitants.

On the occasion of my visit, the only available parking was along the nearby street and there weren't many nearby empty spaces. Given the nature of the clientele, it would seem necessary to convert the space behind and alongside the building into parking spaces.

description:
Rehab First was the most chaotic healthcare provider I’ve ever dealt with. Reports were consistently delayed, communication was poor, and even basic requests often turned into arguments.

When I pointed out that they were being paid for their services, the secretary replied, “Well, your insurance pays us, not you.” That comment summed up the entitled attitude I experienced. I also asked to have an Occupational Therapist removed from my file. They agreed but never followed through. When I brought it up again, the secretary argued with me instead of resolving the issue.

At one point, this same Occupational Therapist called me for a scheduled appointment but, in the middle of the call, said they had to deal with personal matters and ended the conversation abruptly.

In my opinion, this organization is disorganized, unprofessional, and should be avoided.

TLDR; I asked a question about my quote vs my invoice, they didn't respond, then I was later sent a bill with interest by a different person. Also, they were not accommodating with work insurance billing structure.

I received some Occupational Therapy and I was on vacation when the bill arrived. Upon my return I questioned why my invoice & quote were different. I didn't receive a follow-up for 2.5 months, then received an outstanding invoice with interest from a different person. I understand he was on paternal leave but why not check the latest status before essentially sending the invoice off to your internal collections team? Unbelievable.

I asked to get the invoice split up for the different aspects of the job as there was some miscommunication about how my partner's work insurance works. They wouldn't accommodate. Upon answering their questions & politely asking for their assistance with the invoice, I was again sent over to their internal collections team. It was a truly insulting experience. I would not recommend.

I am very pleased and can't thank enough to the whole rehab team that helped me recover. I would especially like to shout out to Janki Satyapanthi, Physiotherapist who is very  professional,  punctual  and has great knowledge about physiotherapy. I would surely recommend her and rehab first to anyone who needs in home services to recover and feel better.

name: YMCA of Owen Sound Grey Bruce

description:
Nothing but the best of service. For a physical, you get a full hour with a nurse practitioner; anything requiring attention gets you a specialist. This place is amazing!

- Resource Workers:
Really helpful, until they're not that helpful.
Was recieving a lot of help from this place in regards to resources until I stopped having my calls or messages answered or returned. Finally a resource worker met with me; I asked for a resource suggestion - no suggestions, no assistance, I asked if the resource people could suggest a mechanic shop, ask around for me, or make a posting to which I did not recieve an answer so the resource people are not resourceful if they can't just send you off with some funding.

Added: Still having to chase staff around for answers on questions asked daysss ago - no call back. I have to nag to get my questions answered.

Have been told by others in the community that this is a common occurance: that the resource workers will do a ton to help at first, then months later, if you ask for anything at all, will most likely brush you off. Basically, take advantage of the help they give you at first, because later you won't even be able to get a returned phone call most of the time. I am not the only one experiencing and complaining of this.

- Mental Health Workers:
Issues with counsellors there, doing their job. It's a problem if you have too many mental health issues and don't fake being okay, they get overwhelmed and let you go as a client. Mainly just print you out paperwork, pretty much useless, just go to CMHA instead.

Added: Had a much better worker after whom has now unfortunatley left the centre.

- Receptionists:
Receptionists there are rude.
One middle age lady, brown hair always tied back, speaks over you before you are done, interrupts and cuts you off, both on the phone and in person. Just transfers calls off, when I told her I am not getting answers or calls back ever, she continued to get pissy with me like that's my issue. You actually get paid, all shift, to deal with these issues and to do your job, no one is paying me to nag you to do your job (call me back).

Added: another issue with the same lady. Booked apt to go in food pantry. Some lady at another location (despite calling the MARKDALE location) told me to go on in and if anyone had an issue with it, to say ____ said to go ahead. I went in and checked at reception where I told her what I was told. She then caused issues for me, stating I needed to book an apt.... which I had called to do already. Don't get sassy with me because your staff can't keep up on what's going on and your phone numbers send me to a different location- that's your issue, not mine; I did what I'm supposed to, thank you, I don't need the unneccessary attitude.

Young brunette receptionist is absolutley phenomenal and always kind, white hair lady is pretty good as well, sorry, I don't know names.

Do your jobs? Resource people are not resourceful, mental health people drop you when there are too many mental health issues you are facing and receptionists can't wait to ship you off to someone else, then get pissy when you call back due to no answer or return call.

- Others:
Not sure Della's position, but she is phenomenal. Cathy is very nice and welcoming. The ladies in the CMHA are beautiful souls, Kelly and Cookie. The workers of the pantry are sweethearts, including two new woman, Cindy and Maham that they have added to the team. There is also have a male worker that helps out with various things around the centre whom is very pleasant to deal with, also.

No complaints about the Drs so far.
Other staff and volunteers are absolutley wonderful, kind and welcoming!

The Newmarket Community Centre & Lions Hall is a well-maintained, versatile facility ideal for various events and gatherings. Located in the heart of Newmarket, the centre is easily accessible, with ample parking nearby and proximity to the vibrant downtown core.

One of its standout features is the range of multipurpose spaces it offers, including large halls, meeting rooms, and breakout areas. The halls are spacious and equipped with modern amenities, making them suitable for weddings, corporate events, parties, and recreational activities. The staff is friendly, professional, and accommodating, ensuring smooth coordination for events of all sizes.

The centre also boasts a scenic location adjacent to the Fairy Lake Park, providing visitors with opportunities to enjoy the natural surroundings. Its outdoor spaces are perfect for photoshoots or for taking a breather during events.

Community programs and recreational activities are regularly hosted here, catering to diverse interests and age groups. From fitness classes to art workshops, the centre fosters a sense of belonging for residents.

While the facility is well-maintained, some visitors have noted that additional signage within the building could improve navigation. Overall, the Newmarket Community Centre is a valuable hub for both private events and community engagement, blending functionality with a welcoming atmosphere.

name: Association pour l'intégration sociale d'Ottawa
address: 235 Donald St, Suite 150 Ottawa, ON, K1K 1N1 (12km) Clipboard
description:
My brother was a resident of this home for a short period of time.  As a resident of the home, it's not usual for residents to have the odd injury due to the interaction between the members. What really concerns me is that any time an injury occurred, the association would downplay the events that caused the injury and often, wanted to report issues verbally only.  One time, one of the caregivers reported that my brother had fallen down the stairs (he holds the walls and railing so he would have had to been pushed).  When we inquired more from the management team, they downplayed it and said he had fallen down the last step.  I understand from their perspective why they would choose to downplay and provide information only verbally - they have to report all incident to the government so keeping incident rate as low as possible is in their best interest.

Unfortunately, my brother died while in the care of AISO and dealing with the association was a nightmare.  AISO was more concerned about protecting their reputation than working with the family members to understand what happened.  It took over 6 weeks for them to sit down with us and explain in detail what happened.  For that to happen, they even wanted to know all our questions in advance so that they could "prep" their answers.  For the family to get any answers, everything had to be on their terms and what they deemed appropriate.  All we wanted was a detailed explanation of what lead to the death of a loved one.   Instead, the AISO made the ordeal painful and they were even caught in a number of lies.

My recommendation is to avoid working with the AISO, even if you feel like you have no choice.  Regardless, always insist on getting everything writing and don't stop asking questions because the AISO will downplay everything to protect themselves.

Great establishment that helps individuals despite physical or mental disabilities to learn life skills according to their abilities

For my part, I had the opportunity to meet some of the staff, witness their interactions, and gain a better understanding of their needs. But what truly touches me is the connection they develop with each new person, driven by a desire to enrich their lives. The staff build relationships with them as if they were family and friends. It's truly beautiful to see and to know that they have opportunities to thrive, stability, the chance to socialize, experience wonderful moments, enjoy life, and be loved—but even more importantly, they deserve it! Furthermore, it's a French-language service in Ontario. Families can be served in the language of their choice. AISO is deeply committed to the well-being of the people they serve.

We have been part of a shared living project for over 15 years. We continually appreciate the enriching experience that the presence of the people we live with has brought us and continues to bring us. Our arrival in this vibrant and stimulating environment has allowed us to support, provide well-being, and foster the development of many people with intellectual disabilities who benefit from this assistance. We, too, have greatly benefited from this entire adventure. The legacy we have left to our children and those around us, the values ​​we have passed on to them, only serve to strengthen our family. Thank you to AISO for allowing us to live and continue to live this wonderful journey.

Thank you, Paule and Luc

name: Collège La Cité

description:
If you have to attend this school, the other option is definitely better, especially if you're investing a lot of your money. The school itself is a bit underwhelming (feels like a high school, honestly), and when it comes to the education you're receiving, it’s a joke. There are some good teachers, but the overall support you get is none. I think choosing this school has been one of the worst mistakes of my life, and I hope others dont make the same mistake.

My experience at La Cité College has been truly rewarding. It's a great place to grow, both personally and professionally. The teachers are amazing—they really understand their students, are always ready to help, and show genuine kindness. The management is also supportive and works hard to create a positive learning environment. I’m grateful to have been part of such a caring and encouraging community.

I've contacted them multiple times only to get tossed around from person to person. Terrible service, terrible schedules, extremely expensive for international students. Worst 4 years of my life and a waste of 13,000$

My only complaint is that the online classes are hard to be interested in, but that’s because they’re online classes and in general they’re not my favorite. Other than that, 10/10, would recommend.

I was planning to enroll in the Explore Program this summer to improve my French — not anymore.

The information on the website is vague and not user-friendly when trying to register. Not all options are available to purchase or clearly explained, even when following the course code numbers. While they list a general price, there’s a note stating, “Temporary residents will have to pay extra fees,” yet no exact amount is provided.

I first emailed the specific account listed for the Explore Program — no response. I followed up with another email a couple of days later — still no answer to this day (2 weeks have passed).

So I started calling the contact number listed for the Explore Program. Unfortunately, the person who answered had no information about the program. Why have a dedicated phone line if the person answering can’t provide any relevant details? I was asked for my email and phone number and was told someone would call me back that same day. No one did.

I called again the next day. Same thing — I gave my contact information and was promised a callback. Once again, nobody contacted me.

On the third day, I called again. This time, they apologized for not getting back to me and promised that someone would definitely call that day. They finally called — the next day — but the person on the line still had no specific information. She couldn’t tell me the price for temporary residents, provide concrete details, or answer my questions. She said she needed to consult a teacher and would send me an email with all the information that same day. That email never arrived.

As someone who was planning to pay out of pocket, this entire process has felt like a bad joke. No one seems to have any knowledge about the program. They promise follow-ups but never deliver. They can’t even provide basic details like the target audience, class size, or whether the focus is on grammar or conversation. It’s been frustrating and stressful.

Just sharing my experience in case someone else is considering this French program. At this point, I won’t try to get more information — it feels like a waste of both time and money.

name: New Leaf Living and Learning Together
address: 19601 Leslie St East Gwillimbury, ON, L0G 1R0 (7km) Clipboard
description:
The staff here are incredibly unorganized and quite useless!

Overall I can’t complain about New Leaf as far as a government subsidized home for people with extreme mental health issues. The only thing that bothers me is the contracting out of residential staff. It seems to me that the contracted staff really don’t care about the clients and only care about doing the bare minimum. I honestly think they only care about getting paid. My son has been here for almost 13 years and the turnover with staff is heartbreaking cause as soon the clients start to get to trust the new staff it’s time for the staff to move on and I can’t imagine the feeling of abandonment that my son and other clients must feel when they leave and it definitely doesn’t help their progress when they have to start learning to trust a new staff member who’s a complete stranger to them. Autistics need a lot of time before they start trusting anyone and unfortunately as soon as they do with the staff at New Leaf, the staff are transferred to somewhere else or promoted and the poor clients have to start learning to trust their replacement. It’s like 2 steps forward and 3 steps back. Unfortunately that’s the state of affairs with housing people with severe mental issues. Lack of funds hurts too. I’m giving New Leafs a 4 star review cause I’ve seen some of the other government run homes for people with autism and I wouldn’t let my cat live there. Overall they really try hard but their hands are tied to a certain degree by the union. But most staff genuinely care about the clients and New Leaf is the best long term care home for people with severe mental health problems who need chronic care that I’ve been able to find. There’s good and bad at every home for people like my son but New Leaf is among the best these days. BUT, they’re unionized so you’re ALWAYS gonna have people who take advantage of the union so they can slack off and be protected by the union. I’d be MUCH happier without the union and I was a union president (UFCW) in the 90s. Final comment: very good but plenty of room for improvement.

Barrister Kingsley really knows his job, he is indeed tried and tested and he has proven to be capable. I highly recommend him.

Absolute joke. The manager is a tyrant and she is still ignorant to all covid 19 precautions putting employees and residents at risk.  Complete joke she is.

My son is well cared for. Thank you

description:
