Wasn't given help. I attended the safe beds program in St Catharines during a crisis which is operated by CMHA. They literally said by the end of it "we don't know how to help you" if you need a place to stay then great, but as far as using it for it's intended purpose which is mental health care, it was yet another battle with combative staff and most of the therapy "sessions" are arguments or them taking notes while you talk and not saying much. Notes that then don't get passed on properly from one shift to the next. If you're coming here tired, depressed and defeated, just know this is yet another place you need to stay on top of the note taking and exhaust yourself with phone calls and they are really just professional Google searchers so don't expect much. I feel more upset on my last day than on my first day. Funding should be pulled and this racket should be closed up. Oh and enjoy lots of bloody snot on the wall next to your bed and no AC at night as they don't control it, it's a shared building, so if sleep deprivation makes your mental health worse, don't come in the summer if you need it chilly to sleep. They do provide fans and have private rooms, but it's not much help. Food is also slop, and if you have a special diet, make sure you check your food before taking a bite as like many things here, communication between staff is poor. Wish I had read the reviews beforehand. I had this place hyped up by a couple friends but found out they meant the Hamilton location, so I can't speak for the whole network but the St. Catharines location is very bad.

A family member of mine has been involved with this "organization " for several years. I have never witnessed such a lack of compassion and incompetence.

CMHA SERVICES OF NIAGARA have helped me immensely; they're always there to lend an ear and have patience, compassion, understanding and great ideas for coping with life's stressors; I highly recommend them if you ever need help and someone understanding ♥️ thank you CMHA!

I have called for help numerous occasions and was hoping each time would be better. I wasn't looking for a miracle, but I was hoping for some kind of improvement! Nothing! I don't know if it's lack of government funding, laziness or lack of empathy! Pathetic!

Horrible help explained my situation, ended up homeless. No addictions don't even smoke cigarettes! Looking for help took my info 6 to 7 weeks ago said someone would follow up within two weeks. So much money goes into paying people to help people but no help in the end. CMHA you keep dropping the ball your funding should go else we're. All these people with all the answers, but they don't even know the question!!!

Rebuilt Resources is a hidden gem in North Bay for anyone who loves thrift shopping! The store is well-organized and offers a wide range of items, including furniture, home goods, kitchenware, clothing, tools, and lots of unique collectibles. Whether you’re furnishing your home on a budget or looking for a rare vintage piece, this place has something for everyone.

The prices are very reasonable, and I found that many items are in great condition. It’s easy to spend an hour or more browsing through the different sections, there’s always something new to discover. The furniture selection especially stood out to be a solid, practical pieces at prices you won’t find anywhere else.

The staff is friendly and helpful, and it’s great to know that the proceeds support a good cause in the community. Highly recommend stopping by if you’re in the area!

description:
The treatment of social services workers towards the disabled and vulnerable community is truly abysmal. Those in mental and physical anguish find these services unreliable, compounding their distress rather than alleviating it. The workers' inability to perform even simple clerical duties often leads to significant issues, creating barriers that prevent individuals from receiving necessary medical treatment. Supposed to be a pillar of support, this service instead fosters disappointment with its blatant lack of sincerity and sympathy. Non-medical professionals are making decisions about patient treatment without the required knowledge or education, akin to a plumber performing a hysterectomy. Such incompetence and deceit are inexcusable, and they exacerbate the challenges faced by those already struggling with a myriad of issues.

Caseworkers are impossible to get ahold of and sub par at best, they seem to think if you make a little too much money your disabilities and related expenses just disappear, they cut your benefits and that extra money you make now goes to pay for your medication and devices so you're no farther ahead. The system is designed to keep you in poverty, and the case workers are apathetic to your needs. You won't find help or empathy here..

Turned me away and sent me to social services without even hearing my situation. Get told at social services after explaining it to them that I SHOULD have gotten help at the odsp office and was completely eligible to start working on odsp paperwork.

Workers here are far too concerned with standing in a circle and continuing their conversation than actually helping those in need. Disgraceful.

Always had polite, efficient and timely service from the Peterborough ODSP office.  I have dealt with them several times over the last few months regarding a family member -- calls are returned promptly, no problems of any kind.

However, I can see that many ODSP recipients could encounter difficulties:  ODSP is a government service/office, as such they follow rather stringent policies and procedures that can be difficult for many to navigate and comply with.  Perhaps too many people consider ODSP to be a social services agency, which it is not.  It is an office that manages disability-related financial assistance.  They are not there to help you find housing, to help you navigate any systems, etc.  They are there to administer a government-funded financial aid program.

Lead to an awkward interview and after the interview i contacted Kristen to tell her it could have obviously gone better but i was confident still, she was very unapologetic and not understanding of what resume services were. I think it was honestly funny to her that i went in there unprepared and she most likely got a good chuckle out of it, seemed malicious to me. The barebones job list EPC does offer (sept-nov 2025) currently seems to consist weekly of nursing jobs and other jobs that require training and or experience i do not have, nor do i have the resources to obtain said training. The job i was interested in was at home hardware and even they wanted experience when they literally hire highschool students. So that is why i wanted my background molded to fit that specific job application and was honestly just looking for advice to rework my resume. In my experience speaking to other clients and managers who hire through EPC pleanty of employers like hire through EPC, only to keep you for three months as cheap labor then get rid of you for almost any reason so they can go get another fresh one. rinse and repeat. Interesting how you guys dont share statistics with clients on how many times an employeer has been looking to fill the exact same position every year. 9 phonecalls spanning 6 hours and nobody is on the same page, some people are mentioning an older procedure and some are saying no they can just close the account. Im not supposed to be the one figuring out your policies and how your backend should work. You guys fighting me on trying to get you to speak to Ageilic is also highly childish. Like i said to make your client pay broken telephone is absolutely ridiculous and maybe you guys need to stay late a few days a week to figure out a better policy. god forbid you took away more "passion" from your already lacking services to those who are pushed your way. If you guys want you could maybe create a temporary job when needed out of this whole issue, internally at one of your offices or rotate to another at agelic and ymca to handle transfers and give people some much needed yet somehow extremely hard to get office experience. If you care about your clients and the community i am positive you can all actually work together and find a better way.

I recently moved to Peterborough, ON. I had zero success applying on my own for almost two years, when I had to leave the last company as they went out of business. I mean 0 interviews of any kind. I also had many gaps in my resume and lacked the confidence to talk about what I could offer.

I spent about a month working with Conrad (about 1 meeting a week), re-working my resume and discussing how best to format and present myself via resume and interview. Conrad was patient and kind at every step, and wanted to understand my point of view completely, so as he could provide the best fit assistance for me. I was able to have brutally honest conversations with him about frustrations and things that had happened in unemployment and hiring processes that you could never tell a recruiter. Then he helped me redefine it all so that I knew what I could say about my experience and how to say it so that I was not shooting myself in the foot.

The first job I applied too, after meeting with Conrad and getting his feedback,  resulted in 1st and 2nd interviews and an offer. Granted this is not going to be the case for everyone, BUT, for me this was not the real value of working with Conrad.

The real value was the way he was able to reignite a drive inside of me to help me keep going. Also, he was able to extinguish the imposter syndrome that I was not good enough, or that somehow I was defined by the text on my resume.

For all of this I will be forever be grateful to Conrad and EPC (Employment Planning & Counselling - Peterborough). From the first call with the staff office member to the last meeting with Conrad, I was met with sincere and genuine respect. Thank you for everything EPC!

I'm part of a team that sponsors refugees.  One of our recent refugees has been going to EPC.  I am very impressed with the quality of service and expertise of all the staff. They are friendly, kind, professional and extremely helpful.  Lana Wierdsma does an incredible with job development.  My friend's confidence and  job  readiness and skill level is increasing ove the last few months through a job placement, job club sessions and the ongoing encouraging and support from Lana.
I so appreciate this valuable service in our town that welcomes and supports newcomers.

name: Agilec

description:
I had a great experience working with Agilec. I worked closely with Devon and Madison, and they were both incredibly kind, respectful, and genuinely helpful throughout the entire process. They took the time to listen, answer questions, and provide support whenever I needed it, which made a huge difference.

What really stood out to me is that Agilec didn’t just help me find work — they invested in my future. The company provided financial assistance so I could obtain certifications, giving me the opportunity to start a career, not just have a job. That level of support and care is something you don’t find everywhere.

I’m very grateful for the guidance and encouragement from Devon, Madison, and the entire Agilec team, and I would highly recommend them to anyone looking to build a meaningful career.

Agelic has truly been life-changing for me. I originally came in looking for help finding work, and I never expected to walk away with a complete career change. Devon and Sarah went above and beyond throughout the entire process. They helped me rebuild my resume, identify my strengths, and guided me toward a new path.

What stood out the most was their genuine support—they aren’t just offering advice, they are actively investing in my future by helping financially support my transition into welding. With their guidance and encouragement, I’ve moved from my old career into a trade that I enjoy and am excited to grow in. I’m incredibly grateful to Devon, Sarah, and the entire Agelic team for believing in me and making this possible. Highly recommend to anyone looking for real support and meaningful career opportunities.

They help you improve and educate yourself with opportunities you may have not had, and resources to push you into the career you are looking for.

name: Peterborough Regional Health Centre

description:
Recent 5-hour wait in the Emergency Department. My observation was that the long wait did not seem to be caused by a staff; in fact, there were many nurses and support staff present. doesn't seem like shortage of staff, However, there was a visible lack of priority regarding patient care.

While some patients are in serious pain, I repeatedly saw groups of staff members chatting and socializing instead of attending to those waiting. It felt as though many were simply working on there relax comfort zone and "clocking in" to finish a shift rather than working with the heart and urgency that emergency care requires.
I want to commend the Senior Nurse and the Doctor, who were both wonderful and professional. I hope their work ethic can serve as an example to the rest of the team. We should all remember that even when a supervisor isn't watching, we have a moral duty to help those who are suffering. I hope this feedback encourages the staff to prioritize their duties and show more empathy toward patients in distress.

It was so hard getting there, i almost got into an accident driving. I wish this all could have been done while i was there. Now i have had an ultrasound and no one has called me. And i cannot move my arm and I am in the most intense pain you could ever imagine. They just gave me pain medication and sent me home, with out proper tests i feel. I just called and a nurse told me to come back and register for my results and that i should probably be seen again? Why did they send me home. I feel this is a serious emergency, all night i could not sleep and was overwhelmed with intense pain.I need to know whats going on with my arm. Im so confused i feel there is some sort of mistake or something missing. Does a manager or someone read these reviews? Something in my process hasnt neen done correctly and I need this to be taken care of correctly.

name: Canadian Mental Health Association - Lambton Kent
address: 210 Lochiel St Sarnia, ON, N7T 4C7 Clipboard
description:
Is this review long? Maybe.
Is it informative? You decide.
Is it from the heart? Most definitely.

I first came to CMHA years ago when I was struggling with stress, anxiety, and other mental health concerns. From the moment I approached the front desk, I felt like the support truly began. I was welcomed, reassured, and treated with respect.

Within minutes, I was introduced to a crisis worker who took the time to understand my feelings and thoughts. As I got to know my crisis worker, it became clear that she—and the others I met—were genuinely there for me.

I’ll admit that as you go through assessments and a treatment plan is developed, it can be difficult to transition between different workers. While each professional is responsible for a specific aspect of your care, it’s still hard to leave those you’ve built a connection with. However, what stood out to me was that, despite these transitions, every single person I encountered was there for one reason: to support those who reached out, even if they showed up unannounced.

The process isn’t rushed. Everything is taken seriously, especially your safety. My treatment plan may not be the same as yours, and my family and friends who have attended CMHA have followed very different paths. But one thing remained consistent—the path chosen with each individual was tailored to their specific needs.

Medication and treatment options are discussed, never forced. You are in control of your care, and your discussions are held in the strictest confidence—unless there is an immediate risk to yourself or others. I encourage you to open up as much as you can because your privacy is respected.

The staff are highly educated and maintain professionalism and boundaries, but above all, they are human first. I didn’t always find a sympathetic ear when discussing my mental health in other places, but at CMHA, they navigated my personality and struggles in a way that made me feel truly heard and safe.

When I was younger, mental health wasn’t openly discussed. It was misunderstood, dismissed, or seen as laziness. CMHA changed that for me. If you have any concerns about your mental health, I encourage you to reach out. You will be supported fully.

I continue to attend CMHA weekly, often bringing friends and family, and I see them finding hope—sometimes for the first time in a long time. I’ve read many reviews about Sarnia’s CMHA, and while perspectives vary, I believe some negative reviews come from those who still need additional support. That’s not to say CMHA is perfect, but I know that my own review would have looked different at the beginning of my journey compared to now.

Mental health care isn’t one-size-fits-all. You won’t know if CMHA is right for you unless you meet with them. If it’s not the right fit, you’re free to walk away. But if there’s even a small chance you connect with the people you meet, I hope you give yourself that opportunity.

Remember: mental health is health. Take care of it.

name: Insight Advantage Inc
address: 595 Tecumseh Rd E, Unit 3 Windsor, ON, N8X 2S1 (3km) Clipboard
description:
We left an online request for contact. No contact was made. Went to the Leamington location during posted hours. No one was there. The Leamington mailbox was full, so we called the Tecumseh location to make a virtual appointment . No link was sent for the appointment. Called and asked for meeting link. Link was sent.  No one showed up for the meeting. Not sure what is going on here, but complete waste of time.

I was gaslit into wasting my time registering with Insight Advantage. I was led to believe I would be able to secure a job through a job developer, but that never happened. While there was communication, there was no follow-up and no real solutions.

Despite asking many times, I received no job fair referrals, no employment leads, and no meaningful support. They respond, but nothing comes of it—your file just sits there with no progress.

If all you want is basic services like resume help or interview training, this might be for you. But if you actually need help finding a job or even getting referred to positions, they are far from helpful. You can get the same generic services from almost any other employment service provider—often with far more concrete support.

Don’t waste your time if you’re serious about finding work. This feels like another waste of government funding with little to no real outcome for clients.

After you register with them and get in their books.They can help you with a bus pass. They help you find work. And they actually look for work for you, as well, even when you're not looking. They can place you in jobs and they're not like a temporary agency, trying to make money off you. So if you're looking for a job, you probably should check this place out. I highly recommend it.

I was overall not pleased with my worker. He admitted on multiple occasions that he has only helped one other individual in applying for BetterJobsOntario. This was very evident in his lack of knowledge and communication. I could have been on my career path a month sooner had he been more organized and had better communication and checked messages. There were also things that he said that could not give someone confidence in getting back into the workforce.

My "employment specialist" was not helpful. I did all the hussle work myself. He tried to divert me away from the field that I just went through school for because he felt jobs in that field weren't available in the city. He tried to discourage me from applying to the hospital because it was "too competitive." He "helped" secure a work placement in the place that I was already doing a school placement and although he spoke to "my boss" he didn't get a name and with an entire management team I was unable to verify who he spoke to or if the information about a position after my placement was in fact true. After a couple weeks it became apparent that the organization was not a good fit for me and I requested assistance in looking for other options but my concerns about the fit were dismissed and no other opportunities were ever presented by him. He kept pushing for me to attend an online course in job searching although I told him that I didn't need that, especially since that was part of the program I was just finishing up at school. He signed me up for that anyway - during a time that I had scheduled mandatory school placement hours that had already been conveyed to him multiple times. I'm pretty sure he never read my emails that I was required to send him because none of the information was noted during the next conversation. All throughout this time I did my own job searching, found several opportunities in my field and did my own networking. I got myself an interview with the hospital and I got hired PT. I never received acknowledgement or congratulations. When I mentioned the possibility of searching for an additional part time job he suggested looking for 1 full-time job. I currently have one of the best PT jobs available in the city and also that someone could get with my level of cirtififcation. This should be something that an employment specialist knows, or should take a few minutes to research.
